work_completed: Finalized initial architecture: language, auth, and primary datastore decisions

decisions.description: Use TypeScript and Node.js across the entire stack — frontend, backend API, and workers
decisions.rationale: Team of four all have TypeScript experience; single language reduces context switching; strong typing catches schema drift early in a pipeline-heavy system

decisions.description: Use JWT tokens for authentication — stateless bearer tokens, 1-hour expiry, RS256 signed
decisions.rationale: Stateless auth scales horizontally without sticky sessions; no session store needed at current scale; RS256 allows key rotation without invalidating all tokens

decisions.description: Use PostgreSQL as the primary datastore
decisions.rationale: ACID guarantees required for billing and event deduplication; team is familiar with Postgres; rich querying for ad-hoc analytics during early product phase

work_completed: Designed event ingestion pipeline: queue, caching strategy, and rate limiting

decisions.description: Use Redis Streams as the internal event queue between ingestion and processing workers
decisions.rationale: Redis already needed for rate limiting; Streams support consumer groups for parallel workers; avoids Kafka operational overhead at current event volume (< 5k events/day)

decisions.description: No dedicated query cache layer — serve all reads directly from Postgres
decisions.rationale: Current Postgres query p95 latency is 180ms at load; adding a cache layer introduces invalidation complexity that is not justified at this scale; revisit if p95 exceeds 1s

decisions.description: Rate limit all public API endpoints at 100 requests per minute per IP using Redis sliding window
decisions.rationale: Redis already in stack; sliding window is more accurate than fixed window for burst protection; 100 req/min is well above legitimate use patterns we have observed in beta

work_completed: Finalized deployment target and CI/CD pipeline

decisions.description: Deploy on a single server using Docker Compose — not Kubernetes
decisions.rationale: No customer has requested HA or horizontal scaling; Kubernetes adds significant ops overhead (cluster management, ingress, node pools) that is not justified for a two-node deployment; Docker Compose is sufficient for current and projected 6-month load

decisions.description: Use GitHub Actions for CI/CD — build, test, and deploy on merge to main
decisions.rationale: Already using GitHub; free tier covers current build volume; no migration cost from an existing CI system

_comment: Session 4 — Feb 28, 2026 — Auth incident response. SUPERSEDES velox_d02_jwt_auth.
work_completed: Replaced JWT auth with server-side sessions following token-theft incident in staging

decisions.description: Replace JWT tokens with server-side sessions stored in Redis, delivered via HttpOnly cookies. This decision supersedes velox_d02_jwt_auth.
decisions.rationale: JWT token theft occurred in staging: attacker obtained a long-lived refresh token via a misconfigured logging endpoint and replayed it after nominal expiry due to a clock skew bug in the expiry check. HttpOnly cookies prevent JavaScript-based token exfiltration (XSS). Server-side sessions allow instant revocation. Tradeoff: requires Redis session store and sticky sessions or session replication, which is acceptable given Redis is already in the stack.
alternatives_considered: opaque tokens with Redis lookup (similar outcome, kept session model instead)

decisions.description: Retain JWT middleware on /v1/legacy/* routes only, with hard expiry cutoff of 2026-04-01
decisions.rationale: Three enterprise integrations depend on JWT bearer tokens; migration window needed; hard cutoff prevents indefinite JWT accumulation

work_completed: Designed per-tenant analytics store and LLM inference setup

decisions.description: Use SQLite as the per-tenant embedded analytics store — separate from the primary Postgres datastore
decisions.rationale: Analytics queries are tenant-scoped and read-heavy; SQLite avoids cross-tenant query interference in the shared Postgres instance; one SQLite file per tenant simplifies tenant offboarding (delete the file); not a replacement for Postgres — used only for the analytics module

decisions.description: Use Ollama (llama3.2) for LLM inference in local development; use Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5) in production
decisions.rationale: Ollama is free and runs offline for fast dev iteration; Claude Haiku is fast enough at p50 < 500ms for production latency requirements and cheaper than Sonnet for high-volume summarization tasks

_comment: Session 6 — Mar 20, 2026 — Performance response. CONTRADICTS velox_d05_no_cache (implicit, not a formal supersession).
work_completed: Added Redis query result cache after load testing revealed Postgres latency regression at scale

decisions.description: Add Redis for query result caching with a 5-minute TTL
decisions.rationale: Load testing at 500 concurrent users showed Postgres query p95 latency at 2.3 seconds — well above the 1-second threshold set in the Jan 25 no-cache decision. The Postgres query plan cache is not sufficient under this write-heavy concurrent load pattern. Redis is already in the stack for Streams and rate limiting, so no new infrastructure is added.

decisions.description: Invalidate cache entries on any write to the same project_id — do not rely on TTL expiry alone
decisions.rationale: TTL-only invalidation would serve stale query results for up to 5 minutes after new events are ingested; for a decision-tracking product, stale answers erode trust; write-through invalidation is simpler than versioning

_comment: Session 7 — Apr 5, 2026 — Enterprise scaling. SUPERSEDES velox_d07_docker_compose.
work_completed: Migrated deployment target to Kubernetes on AWS EKS to meet enterprise customer SLA

decisions.description: Migrate from Docker Compose to Kubernetes for production deployment. This decision supersedes velox_d07_docker_compose.
decisions.rationale: Acme Corp (first enterprise customer) requires a 99.9% uptime SLA and horizontal scaling to handle their 50k daily active user spike. Docker Compose cannot support rolling deployments without downtime or auto-scaling. Kubernetes satisfies both requirements.

decisions.description: Use AWS EKS (managed Kubernetes) rather than self-managed Kubernetes
decisions.rationale: No one on the team has Kubernetes ops experience; managed control plane reduces operational burden; Acme Corp is already on AWS so data residency is simpler; EKS Fargate mode eliminates node pool management
